assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 5: 'Workflow Mode: `feature-branch`' with rationale listing three atomicity indicators: 'Coordinated schema migration', 'Breaking API changes (internal)', and 'Tightly coupled feature components'. Each indicator explains why partial merges would break the application.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence:
task-1-create-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type
create-branch' (lines 7-8). It is task 1, the first task in the plan.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence:
task-7-merge-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type
merge-branch' (lines 7-8). It is task 7, the last task in the plan (7 tasks total).

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence:
All intermediate tasks (2-6) have '## Target Branch
TC-9005': task-2 line 8, task-3 line 8, task-4 line 8, task-5 line 8, task-6 line 8.

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
All intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency
assertion_results.evidence: Task 2 (line 61): 'Depends on: Task 1'. Task 3 (line 65): 'Depends on: Task 1'. Task 4 (line 58): 'Depends on: Task 1'. Task 5 (line 48): 'Depends on: Task 1'. Task 6 (line 48): 'Depends on: Task 1'. All five intermediate tasks list Task 1 (create-branch) as a dependency.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md lines 34-39 list dependencies on Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5, and Task 6 — all five intermediate tasks are listed.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md (bookend create-branch) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. task-7-merge-feature-branch.md (bookend merge-branch) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. Intermediate tasks 2-6 all contain the required sections.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any mention of applying a 'workflow:feature-branch' label to the feature issue. The impact-map.md mentions 'Workflow Mode: `feature-branch`' but does not reference applying a label. None of the task files mention a label either.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any reference to 'sha256', 'digest', '[sdlc-workflow]', or any description digest comment. No evidence of SHA-256 hash computation or posting of digest comments after task creation.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any 'Applies: task modifies' rationale format or any reference to convention-applicability-rules.md. While task files reference docs/constraints.md sections (e.g., '§2 Commit Rules', '§5 Code Change Rules'), these are free-form prose references, not the prescribed 'Applies:' format. No evidence of systematic convention applicability validation.
